item.title: Forestry Sector Money Laundering Risk Assessment presents a devastating picture of corruption
item.description: [Act Now PNG] A Money Laundering Risk Assessment for the Forestry Sector published by the Bank of Papua New Guinea presents a devastating picture of large-scale corruption, fraud and illegality in the logging industry.

item.title: Bougainville News Alert : Statement from the Office of the President: Response to Chief John L Momis, GCL, former President of Bougainville
item.description: [Bougainville News] It is disappointing to see a leader of John Momis’ stature, a former two term President of Bougainville and longtime Regional Member for Bougainville trying to undermine the good work of the Autonomous Bougainville Government. See Momis Press Release Part … Continue reading →

item.title: Investing in Papua New Guinea: five things to watch in 2026
item.description: [Business Advantage PNG] In what is shaping up to be a big year for the Papua New Guinea economy, Business Advantage PNG lists five key focus areas to look out for in the country's investment space in 2026. The post Investing in Papua New Guinea: five things to watch in 2026 appeared first on Business Advantage PNG .

item.title: USP Concludes Laucala Graduation with a strong focus on Pacific Leadership and Legacies
item.description: [Education News PNG] The University of the South Pacific (USP) concluded its two-day graduation ceremonies at the Laucala Campus in Suva, Fiji, today, marking another important milestone as The post USP Concludes Laucala Graduation with a strong focus on Pacific Leadership and Legacies first appeared on Education News P

item.title: National Court Overturns Ombudsman Order Blocking Starlink Licence in PNG
item.description: [Inside PNG] PNG’s National Court quashes the Ombudsman Commission’s "ultra vires" order, clearing the legal path for NICTA to resume Starlink’s satellite license review. The post National Court Overturns Ombudsman Order Blocking Starlink Licence in PNG first appeared on Inside PNG . The post National Court Over

item.title: PNG Hunters Crushed by Bears in Heavy Gold Coast Defeat
item.description: [One Papua New Guinea] Papua New Guinea’s SP PNG Hunters suffered a crushing loss on the Gold Coast, going down 42-4 to the Bears in a one-sided Round 7 clash on Saturday. PNG Hunters Crushed by Bears in Heavy Gold Coast Defeat The Hunters struggled from the opening whistle, with the home side piling on early pressure to

item.title: Community Health Workers in PNG
item.description: [Pawa TV] Community health workers (CHWs) are vital in Papua New Guinea's rural health care, offering essential services, promoting health education, and bridging gaps despite facing challenges like limited resources and training opportunities. The post Community Health Workers in PNG first appeared on PAWA T

item.title: PNG50 Gospel Music Festival set for August
item.description: [PNG Buzz] Jubilee Reset Festival: A Nation Gathers to Worship, Pray, and Reset for the Future As Papua New Guinea approaches its 50th year of Independence, a powerful wave of faith and unity is building across the nation. At the heart of this movement is the Jubilee Reset Gospel Music Festival, taking place o

item.title: PNG Strengthens Commitment to Ending Violence Against Women During UN Engagement
item.description: [PNG Facts] Papua New Guinea has reaffirmed its determination to address violence against women and girls, with NCD Governor Powes Parkop outlining key national priorities during a meeting with a visiting United Nations leader in Port Moresby. PNG Strengthens Commitment to Ending Violence Against Women During U

item.title: One Netball PNG reaches rural Milne Bay communities despite challenges
item.description: [Post Courier] ONE Netball PNG has completed an outreach program in Milne Bay Province, overcoming difficult weather conditions to deliver impactful community and youth engagement activities. The team has since returned safely from Alotau after travelling extensively across the province, including a journey from t

item.title: PM opens Tari BSP Branch, IRC Office, Enopi Police Barracks
item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] Prime Minister and Member for Tari-Pori, James Marape, officiated at the opening of major developments aimed at improving banking services, government revenue administration, and law and order in Hela Province. Mr Marape led a high-level delegation for the official opening of the new Bank South Paci

item.title: Governor Parkop commends appointment of ACP Benjamin Turi, welcomes stability in Police leadership
item.description: [The PNG Sun] National Capital District Governor, Powes Parkop has commended Police Minister Sir John Pundari and Police Commissioner David Manning for confirming Benjamin Turi as the permanent Assistant Commissioner of Police (ACP) for NCD and Central Province. In a statement, Governor Parkop described the appoi

item.title: Graun bruk katim Sepik Haiwe
item.description: [Wantok Niuspepa] Bikpela ren i mekim graun i bruk na bagarapim rot long Sepik Haiwe. Dispela rot i save bungim Is na Wes Sepik na i go olgeta long boda bilong PNG wantaim Indonesia.

item.title: ACT NOW goes to court to secure publication of information on the forest industry
item.description: [Act Now PNG] ACT NOW! is taking legal action against the PNG Forest Authority to secure the release of information about large-scale logging operations.

item.title: Bougainville News Alert : A Nation In His Making – A Memory
item.description: [Bougainville News] FIVE YEARS ago we farewelled GC Sir Michael Somare with the most deserving tributes around the country. We also paused to remember him as someone whose political life is synonymous with the birth of PNG as a nation state … Continue reading →

item.title: A day at Port Moresby’s National Museum and Art Gallery
item.description: [Business Advantage PNG] Kate Webster reports that recent renovations to Port Moresby’s National Museum and Art Gallery have revived a cultural heartbeat. The post A day at Port Moresby’s National Museum and Art Gallery appeared first on Business Advantage PNG .

item.title: USP honours more than 60 high achievers at Medals and Prizes Ceremony
item.description: [Education News PNG] More than 60 outstanding students and graduates of The University of the South Pacific (USP) were recognised for academic excellence at the Medals and Prizes The post USP honours more than 60 high achievers at Medals and Prizes Ceremony first appeared on Education News PNG .

item.title: Bula Round? How Fiji can host Super Rugby’s big weekend
item.description: [Inside PNG] Christchurch's Super Round rebirth will give rugby a timely boost as rival destinations, including Fiji, jostle for a piece of the action. The post Bula Round? How Fiji can host Super Rugby’s big weekend first appeared on Inside PNG . The post Bula Round? How Fiji can host Super Rugby’s big weekend

item.title: PNG Receives Emergency Relief Support from New Zealand
item.description: [One Papua New Guinea] Papua New Guinea has received a boost in its disaster recovery efforts as New Zealand delivers emergency relief supplies to communities affected by Tropical Cyclone Maila in East New Britain. The assistance arrived in Rabaul earlier this week aboard a Royal New Zealand Air Force C-130J Hercules, car

item.title: Freshwater Fish Farming
item.description: [Pawa TV] Aquaculture in Papua New Guinea is rapidly expanding, enhancing food security and incomes through community-supported fish farming. While challenges persist, initiatives aim to cultivate a sustainable industry for rural development. The post Freshwater Fish Farming first appeared on PAWA TV .

item.title: PNG NRL Competition finalise Club Line-Up for 2025 Season
item.description: [PNG Buzz] The PNG NRL Competition has announced that the 12 existing teams will compete for the top spot in the 2025 Digicel ExxonMobil Cup, starting in April of 2025. After thoroughly reviewing the 2024 season and assessing the potential for expansion, it was concluded that the competition in 2025 will featu

item.title: U.S. Embassy and PNGUoT Renew Partnership for American Space Lae
item.description: [PNG Facts] Papua New Guinea has reaffirmed its educational connection with the United States following the renewal of a partnership between the U.S. Embassy in Papua New Guinea and the Papua New Guinea University of Technology for the American Space in Lae. U.S. Embassy in PNG and PNGUoT Renew American Space L

item.title: Jarome Luai witnesses grit and passion as Enga Mioks edge Wigmen in Port Moresby
item.description: [Post Courier] What a way to show our prestigious visitor how rugby league is loved and played in PNG! Sir John Guise Stadium reached a boiling point this afternoon as Wests Tigers superstar Jarome Luai made a surprise appearance, stopping the show during the Round 3 Digicel ExxonMobil Cup double-header. The four-

item.title: OPIC Chief Unveils First-Ever Annual Performance Report, Pushes Historic Reform of Oil Palm Sector
item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] General Secretary of the Oil Palm Industry Corporation (OPIC), Mr. Kepson Pupita, has outlined an ambitious plan to institutionalize Papua New Guinea’s oil palm sector through a new legislative framework aimed at transforming the industry into a fully-fledged regulatory and development authority. Sp

item.title: PM Marape welcomes strong IRC revenue growth as PNG enters 50th year
item.description: [The PNG Sun] Prime Minister, James Marape has welcomed the strong revenue performance recorded by the Internal Revenue Commission (IRC) in the first quarter of 2026. He described it as clear evidence that Papua New Guinea’s economy is strengthening through deliberate reforms and disciplined management as the nat

item.title: Gavman Minista bam long kar long haiwe
item.description: [Wantok Niuspepa] Minista bilong 'Energy' na Memba bilong Kairuku, Peter Namea Isoaimo i bin bam long kar long 2nd Februeri, 2026 long Hiritano Haiwe. Em i bam long kar wantaim wanpela soldia bilong PNG Defence Force (PNGDF). Ol kar bilong tupela man i stap nau long polis stesin long 15 Mile na wok painimaut i gohet

item.title: Who's monitoring the social impacts of PNG's mining industry?
item.description: [Act Now PNG] The following article is about the social impacts of the mining industry in the country. According to the writer, inequality and environmental damage remain stubborn features of the sector although Papua New Guinea has never lacked ideas about how to manage these impacts.

item.title: Bougainville News Alerts : Simon Pentanu : My memories of Kieta
item.description: [Bougainville News] The first permanent German presence in Kieta, Bougainville, began with the establishment of a Marist mission in 1901, followed by an official German colonial administrative office in 1905 to oversee the region. They would have seen, steaming into the deep natural harbour, … Continue reading →

item.title: Six questions about greylisting that every Papua New Guinean business exec should be asking
item.description: [Business Advantage PNG] In February, the Financial Action Task Force added Papua New Guinea to its “grey list” of jurisdictions under increased monitoring. In this exclusive opinion piece for Business Advantage PNG , John Burke, an expert in AML/CTF compliance for PNG, shares how businesses can maintain their banking relat

item.title: Australia Awards offers development grants for colleges
item.description: [Education News PNG] Teachers and nursing colleges in Papua New Guinea (PNG) have the opportunity to apply for Institutional Development Grants (IDG) up to K150,000.00 from Australia Awards. The post Australia Awards offers development grants for colleges first appeared on Education News PNG .

item.title: How extreme Aussie weather is fuelling ‘eco-anxiety’
item.description: [Inside PNG] More than half of Australians say they are giving up on regular hobbies such as gardening and playing sport due to stress brought on by extreme weather. The post How extreme Aussie weather is fuelling ‘eco-anxiety’ first appeared on Inside PNG . The post How extreme Aussie weather is fuelling ‘eco-a

item.title: PNG welcomes Türkiye’s first ambassador in historic diplomatic step
item.description: [One Papua New Guinea] Papua New Guinea Prime Minister James Marape has officially received Türkiye’s first resident ambassador to PNG, Her Excellency Bilgin Ozkan, marking a new chapter in diplomatic relations between the two countries. PNG Prime Minister Hon. James Marape MP formally welcomed the first Turkish Ambassado

item.title: Table Tennis
item.description: [Pawa TV] Table tennis is an underrated sport in Papua New Guinea, steadily gaining recognition through impressive performances in regional competitions, including a historic silver and bronze at the 2025 Pacific Mini Games. The post Table Tennis first appeared on PAWA TV .

item.title: Operation Render Safe set to begin in East New Britain
item.description: [PNG Buzz] This week, Governor Michael Marum and Provincial Administrator Levi Mano welcomed Australian High Commissioner John Feakes, Defence Adviser Colonel Travis Gordon, and a team from the Australian Defence Force (ADF) in East New Britain. This visit marks the beginning of a series to the province, as th

item.title: PNG PM Reaffirms Media Freedom at Post-Courier Facility Opening
item.description: [PNG Facts] Papua New Guinea Prime Minister James Marape has reaffirmed his government’s strong stance on protecting press freedom while opening new facilities for the country’s leading newspaper in Port Moresby. Speaking at the launch of Namaliu Haus and Sir Rabbie Haus, developments undertaken by South Pacifi

item.title: Voter Enrollment will not improve the quality of electoral rolll
item.description: [Post Courier] The Planned PNG Electoral Commission Voter Enrollment Program will not improve the quality of the electrol roll. The paper roll have outlive its use to date , futhermore it is a outdated election management tool. The Registrar of Political Party and Candidates Emmanuel Pok alluded to the facts when

item.title: Cook Islands U-16 secure top spot after grinding draw with PNG in OFC championship
item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] Cook Islands have secured top spot in the qualifying tournament and progression to the OFC U-16 Women’s Championship after grinding out a 1-1 draw with Papua New Guinea in Port Moresby. Heading into the clash, both sides were level with one win apiece after dominant performances against American Sam

item.title: Governor Parkop reaffirms commitment to women and girls as UN Under-Secretary-General and UNFPA Executive Director visits PNG
item.description: [The PNG Sun] NCD Governor Powes Parkop has reassured the visiting United Nations Under- Secretary- General and UNFPA Executive Director of the NCD’s commitment to improving the lives of women and girls in Papua New Guinea. Governor Parkop announced the commitment in his keynote address during today’s meeting wit

item.description: [Act Now PNG] ACT NOW! is taking legal action against the PNG Forest Authority to secure the release of information about large-scale logging operations.

item.description: [Bougainville News] President Toroama confirms ABG position on Panguna Partnering process The President of the Autonomous Bougainville Government (ABG), Hon. Ishmael Toroama, has confirmed that the ABG has rejected the proposed partnership between Bougainville Copper Limited (BCL) and CMOC Group Limited in … Continue r

item.title: Nasfund CEO Rajeev Sharma on the super fund’s outstanding annual results… and what’s next
item.description: [Business Advantage PNG] Nasfund had an outstanding year in 2025, with net profit after tax increasing by 28 per cent to K1.09 billion and membership numbers up 4 per cent to 744,213. Nasfund CEO Rajeev Sharma speaks with Business Advantage PNG about the key drivers of this performance – and what’s ahead for the Papua New G

item.title: Term 2 Delay in Manus
item.description: [Education News PNG] The Manus Provincial Education Board (PEB) has issued a delay notice for the start of term 2 due to severe weather. In a memorandum to The post Term 2 Delay in Manus first appeared on Education News PNG .

item.title: Ok Tedi Welcomes New Graduates to 2026 Development Scheme
item.description: [Inside PNG] Ok Tedi Mining Limited welcomes 44 graduates into its 2026 development program, including seven top scholars from Unitech for a future-ready PNG. The post Ok Tedi Welcomes New Graduates to 2026 Development Scheme first appeared on Inside PNG . The post Ok Tedi Welcomes New Graduates to 2026 Developm

item.title: PNG Government Eyes Stronger U.S. Partnership in Energy and Disaster Preparedness
item.description: [One Papua New Guinea] Prime Minister James Marape has signalled Papua New Guinea’s intention to further deepen its partnership with the United States after holding talks with U.S. diplomat Alex Berengberg in Port Moresby. PNG Government Eyes Stronger U.S. Partnership in Energy and Disaster Preparedness Key issues discuss

item.title: The Glassman Bill in PNG
item.description: [Pawa TV] The Glassman Bill, passed in 2022, criminalises sorcery accusations in Papua New Guinea. While arrests and convictions have followed, weak enforcement and limited awareness in some provinces continue to challenge efforts to curb violence. The post The Glassman Bill in PNG first appeared on PAWA TV .

item.title: PNG Delegation learns from Madagascar’s Vanilla Industry
item.description: [PNG Buzz] A delegation from Papua New Guinea, including senior officers from central government agencies such as the Department of National Planning and Monitoring (DNPM), the Spice Industry Board (SIB) under the Department of Agriculture and Livestock (DAL), as well as Provincial Government and Administratio

item.title: New Zealand Delivers Urgent Aid to PNG’s Cyclone-Hit Communities
item.description: [PNG Facts] Papua New Guinea is receiving urgent humanitarian support from New Zealand as recovery efforts continue in East New Britain after Tropical Cyclone Maila left widespread damage across the province. Emergency supplies were flown into Rabaul this week, marking a key step in assisting affected residents

item.title: PNG Fassion Week goes global “Couture In Paradise” in Brisbane
item.description: [Post Courier] In a landmark moment for Papua New Guinea’s creative industry, PNG Fashion Week will stage its first-ever international event, Couture in Paradise, at the iconic Brisbane Multicultural Arts Centre on Friday, 22 May 2026. This highly anticipated showcase marks a powerful new chapter for the organisat

item.title: Students told to provide own chairs and tables
item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] By JOE GURINA A primary school in Central province has forced students to provide tables and chairs due to shortage of these learning infrastructures currently faced by the school. Sogeri primary school in the Hiri Koiari district is facing a serious dilemma with the shortage of important learning i

item.title: Digitec ICT Ltd awarded Scale Computing international partner of the year
item.description: [The PNG Sun] Digitec ICT Limited has been named International Partner of the Year by Scale Computing, Chief Executive Officer, Mr. Satyen Singh announced yesterday during a media conference held at the company’s headquarters in Port Moresby. Scale Computing is a global leader in edge computing and hyperconverged

item.title: PMGH laik ol famili rausim bodi long mog hariap
item.description: [Wantok Niuspepa] Sif Ekseketiv Opisa bilong Pot Mosbi Jeneral Haus Sik, Dokta Paki Molumi i singaut long ol pipel long go sekim mog na rausim dai bodi bilong ol famili na wantok bilong ol hariap, na karim i go planim. Mog i pulap na wanem ol bodi i stap longtaim bai haus sik i bungim olgeta na planim long wanpela bi

item.title: Building a Nation on Land and People
item.description: [Act Now PNG] In 1974, as Papua New Guinea prepared for Self-Government and Independence, the country faced an important choice. It could continue with the colonial system, central control, foreign companies, and fast economic growth that mainly benefited outsiders. Or it could choose a different path.

item.title: Bougainville News Alert 2026 : Simon Pentanu : My meeting with Francis Ona
item.description: [Bougainville News] Meeting Francis Ona – from my Panguna journals. My six year appointment as Chief Ombudsman that began in January 1995 ended on 31 December 2000. This time was also the end of my national public service career. It was … Continue reading →

item.description: [Business Advantage PNG] New Chief Risk Officer at MRDC, new directors at BSP and Nasfund, new General Manager at Pacific Palms Property, new PNG High Commissioner to Fiji, and new UN Resident Coordinator in Papua New Guinea. The post People Moves: MRDC, BSP, Nasfund, Pacific Palms Property, PNG High Commission to Fiji, UN

item.title: USP commits $1 million in student support amid global crisis
item.description: [Education News PNG] The University of the South Pacific (USP) has today announced a $F1 million student hardship and bursary support package to protect students and their families The post USP commits $1 million in student support amid global crisis first appeared on Education News PNG .

item.title: ABG launches Bougainville Wings, region’s new flag carrier
item.description: [Inside PNG] The Autonomous Bougainville Government has launched Bougainville Wings Limited, describing it as Bougainville’s flag carrier and a milestone for the region’s aviation sector. The post ABG launches Bougainville Wings, region’s new flag carrier first appeared on Inside PNG . The post ABG launches Boug

item.title: PNG Police Strengthened Through Regional AFP Leadership Training
item.description: [One Papua New Guinea] Papua New Guinea police officers have joined counterparts from across the Pacific in an intensive leadership training program in Cairns, Australia, aimed at strengthening frontline policing and regional cooperation. PNG Police Strengthened Through Regional AFP Leadership Training/Photo by AFP The in

item.title: The Traditional Sago Making Process in PNG
item.description: [Pawa TV] Sago, a crucial staple in Papua New Guinea, plays a significant role in food security and cultural identity, fostering community collaboration and preserving traditional skills amidst modern influences and changing diets. The post The Traditional Sago Making Process in PNG first appeared on PAWA TV

item.title: Bougainville chocolate festival 2023 underway
item.description: [PNG Buzz] The 2023 annual Bougainville Chocolate Festival is now underway, held over three days from the 26 to 28 September at the Hutjena Oval in Buka. It will be the largest event since the inaugural festival in 2016. The theme of this year’s festival is “Innovation and Information Sharing to Empower Farmer

item.title: PNG Strengthens Ties with United States on Fuel Security and Disaster Support
item.description: [PNG Facts] Papua New Guinea Prime Minister James Marape has reaffirmed the country’s close working relationship with the United States following talks held in Port Moresby with senior U.S. diplomat Alex Berengberg. The discussions focused on deepening cooperation in key sectors, with both sides exploring ways

item.title: Tokome appointed CEO of Lloyds Metals and Energy Limited for its Pacific initiatives
item.description: [Post Courier] Longest serving Puma Energy Chairman Hulala Tokome has been appointed as Chief Executive Officer of Lloyds Metals and Energy Limited for its Pacific initiatives. Lloyds Metals and Energy Limited, India (LMEL) had announced the appointment of Hulala Tokome as Chief Executive Officer for its Pacific i

item.title: Hiri Koiari DDA signs electricity deal with PNG Power, contractor
item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] By JOE GURINA THE Hiri Koiari District Development Authority in Central Province, through chairman and MP Keith Iduhu, signed an electricity deal this week. The signing of a Memorandum of Agreement between PNG Power, contractor TLE and the Hiri Koiari DDA empowers the DDA:s rural electrification pro

item.title: Global spotlight on PNG as UNFPA director champions fight against gender-based violence
item.description: [The PNG Sun] The Executive Director of the United Nations Population Fund (UNFPA), Ms. Diene Keita, is in Port Moresby, Papua New Guinea from 23 to 24 April 2026. She will be meeting with women and communities and highlight the country’s efforts to end gender-based violence, strengthen sexual and reproductive he

item.title: Australia pasim tingting bilong PNG wantaim ragbi lig
item.description: [Wantok Niuspepa] Australia i bilip ol i pasim tingting na bel bilong Papaua Niugini pinis taim ol i sapotim PNG long putim wanpela tim insait long NRL. Ol i bilip dispela i pasim PNG na i no wari long ol arapela wokbung PNG i gat wantaim ol arapela kantri olsem China.

item.title: New Ireland coastal waters causing fish deaths, human sickness
item.description: [Act Now PNG] For several months, the waters around New Ireland province in Papua New Guinea have been causing illness, skin irritation and the death of sea life, according to communities living along the east coast of the island.

item.title: Bougainville News Alerts :Rio Tinto urged to accelerate action on remediation of Panguna mine disaster, one year on from investigation
item.description: [Bougainville News] One year on from the release of an independent investigation into Rio Tinto’s former Panguna mine in Bougainville, communities living with the ongoing environmental impact are calling on the company to urgently move towards funding solutions, particularly in areas identified … Continue reading →

item.title: Cash crop production sends Papua New Guinea’s shipping volumes soaring
item.description: [Business Advantage PNG] Papua New Guinea’s two main international ports enjoyed a strong year in 2025. Capital spending programs are focused on adding capacity in order to prepare for further economic growth, sector leaders tell Business Advantage PNG . The post Cash crop production sends Papua New Guinea’s shipping volume

Divine Word University in PNG Strengthens Training and Student Life The university officially opened the facilities this week,

item.title: By-Elections
item.description: [Pawa TV] By-elections fill vacant legislative seats between general elections, ensuring continued representation. They arise from resignations or deaths and can shift legislative power or reflect public opinion, thus affecting governance. The post By-Elections first appeared on PAWA TV .

item.title: Agripreneurs empowered, MSMEs boosted in Sepik Region
item.description: [PNG Buzz] Under the EU-STREIT PNG programme, FAO equips local entrepreneurs with knowledge, skills and practical tools needed to drive inclusive sustainable development for MSMEs in the Sepik region. Wewak, Papua New Guinea – The Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations (FAO), as part of the EU

item.title: Divine Word University in PNG Expands Student Facilities
item.description: [PNG Facts] Divine Word University in Papua New Guinea has strengthened its commitment to quality education with the opening of two new facilities at its St Benedict Wewak Campus this week. The university unveiled the projects during a formal ceremony attended by government leaders and the academic community. T

item.title: Police Minister pays tribute as Southern Highlands mourns passing of PPC Tuman
item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] Police Minister Sir John Pundari has paid tribute to Southern Highlands Provincial Police Commander (PPC) Superintendent Mas Tuman who passed away at the Mendi General Hospital yesterday. He said policing was a noble but inherently risky profession. “It is a calling that requires a unique brand of c

item.title: PM Marape declares support for freedom of speech, expression and independent media at opening of Post Courier’s new facilities
item.description: [The PNG Sun] Prime Minister, James Marape yesterday declared his government’s unwavering commitment to freedom of speech, freedom of expression, and an independent media while officially opening the Post-Courier’s new staff accommodation and office facilities in Port Moresby. Prime Minister Marape was the keynot

item.title: K30.9 bilien baset
item.description: [Wantok Niuspepa] Gavman i tokaut long K30.9 bilien baset bilong kantri long 2026 tasol em bai no inap yet long bungim ol nid na laik bilong ol manmeri.

item.title: Kastom Giraun celebrates the importance of customary land
item.description: [Act Now PNG] Kastom Giraun is the title of a new book and website launched by ACT NOW! to help teach young people on the value and importance of customary land.

item.title: Bougainville News Alerts : OBEC returns 45 writs, marks completion of 2025 ABG General Election
item.description: [Bougainville News] Bougainville Electoral Commissioner, Desmond Tsianai, today formally returned 45 writs comprising the Presidency, 38 single-member constituencies and the six reserved regional seats for women and Former Combatants. Tsianai formally handed the election writs to the outgoing Speaker, Simon Pentanu, of

item.title: Economic update: Papua New Guinea’s ‘year of reset’
item.description: [Business Advantage PNG] With improved export receipts and the prospect of major resources projects starting in 2026, Papua New Guinea's economy is set for a sustained period of growth. Andrew Wilkins speaks with business leaders in the country about their expectations for the year ahead. The post Economic update: Papua New

item.description: [Inside PNG] Bougainville landowners protest plans to reopen Panguna Mine with Indian firm Lloyds Metals, citing lack of consultation and sparking fresh tension. item.title: PNG Police Operation Targets Crime, Seizes Weapons in Port Moresby
item.description: [One Papua New Guinea] More than 1,000 knives have been taken off the streets in PNG’s capital following an intensified police operation aimed at cleaning up illegal activities in Port Moresby. The enforcement drive, conducted over a three-week period, has seen police move aggressively to remove threats and restore order

item.title: The PNG-US Visa Bond
item.description: [Pawa TV] Papua New Guinea's inclusion in a US visa bond pilot program raises concerns over increased travel costs for citizens, with significant financial requirements potentially limiting opportunities for students and tourists. The post The PNG-US Visa Bond first appeared on PAWA TV .

item.title: Showcasing PNG to the world: 2023 Goroka Show a Success
item.description: [PNG Buzz] Papua New Guinea’s rich cultural heritage came alive once again as the 67th Goroka Show took centre stage at the National Sports Institute (NSI) in Goroka, Eastern Highlands Province. Held over the long weekend commemorating PNG’s 48th Independence, this year’s event, sponsored by the National Gamin

item.title: PNG Moves Toward Local Fuel Production with Biodiesel Plan
item.description: [PNG Facts] A major step toward energy independence has been taken in Papua New Guinea following Cabinet approval of a large-scale biodiesel project spanning Pomio in East New Britain and parts of Central Province. MP Elias Kapavore The endorsement by the National Executive Council signals a shift in policy dir

item.title: Wests Tigers star Jarome Luai spotted in Port Moresby
item.description: [Post Courier] The race for the PNG Chiefs’ inaugural NRL roster has taken a stunning turn with Wests Tigers superstar Jarome Luai spotted on the ground in the nation’s capital today. ​The three-time Premiership-winning halfback, currently the face of the Wests Tigers’ 2026 campaign, is reportedly in town to meet

item.title: USA, PNG sign health agreement to combat infectious diseases
item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] THE United States and Papua New Guinea signed a five-year health cooperation Memorandum of Understanding today, expanding the Trump Administration’s America First Global Health Strategy into the Pacific region. Under the MOU, the United States, working with Congress, intends to provide up to $15 mil

item.title: PM Marape orders full investigation into PNGDF audit findings, commits to stronger defense force
item.description: [The PNG Sun] An audit summary of the PNGDF has shown that while the approved PNGDF establishment stands at over 5,000 positions, only 3,057 positions are currently filled, with 2,186 positions vacant, highlighting a significant shortfall in manpower. Prime Minister and Minister for Defence, James Marape, has ord

item.title: Bird sanap
item.description: [Wantok Niuspepa] Gavana bilong Is Sepik provins i sanap makim oposisen long traim na winim James Marape long kamap Praim Minista insait long 'Vot i no gat bilip'.

item.title: Kastom Giraun: Celebrating the importance of customary land
item.description: [Act Now PNG] Kastom Giraun: New book and website celebrate the importance of customary land

item.title: Bougainville News Alerts : Could tourism lead Bougainville into the future ?
item.description: [Bougainville News] Bougainville has gone through many turbulent periods over the past 100 years, not least the Civil War of the 1990s, which claimed as many as 20,000 lives. The government is now looking to develop the economy and has its eye … Continue reading →

item.title: On the fuel shock, Papua LNG progress and strong super fund results
item.description: [Business Advantage PNG] Papua New Guinea's fuel supplies and prices are under pressure, more positive signs emerge on Papua LNG, and superannuation funds deliver the goods. The post On the fuel shock, Papua LNG progress and strong super fund results appeared first on Business Advantage PNG .

item.title: Pacific Academy Unveils AI-Powered Research Platform
item.description: [Education News PNG] APIA, SAMOA – 16 February 2026 – The Pacific Academy of Sciences (PAS) today announced two historic milestones for Pacific research and innovation at its The post Pacific Academy Unveils AI-Powered Research Platform first appeared on Education News PNG .

item.title: 16kg Marijuana Shipment Intercepted at Jacksons Airport
item.description: [Inside PNG] A passenger traveling from Mt Hagen, Western Highlands Province was arrested and charged for smuggling 16kg of marijuana into Port Moresby. The post 16kg Marijuana Shipment Intercepted at Jacksons Airport first appeared on Inside PNG . The post 16kg Marijuana Shipment Intercepted at Jacksons Airport

item.title: PNG Hunters edge Jets in tense finish at Santos Stadium
item.description: [One Papua New Guinea] Papua New Guinea’s SP Hunters held their nerve to grind out a narrow 26-24 win over the Ipswich Jets in a gripping Round 6 clash at Santos National Football Stadium in Port Moresby on Saturday. The home side clawed their way back after trailing at halftime, showing resilience and control in the seco

item.title: A Decade of Rising Fuel Prices
item.description: [Pawa TV] Fuel prices in Papua New Guinea have increased over the past decade due to global events, particularly the Russia-Ukraine war and Middle East tensions, impacting local costs significantly despite the country's reliance on imported energy. The post A Decade of Rising Fuel Prices first appeared on PAW

item.title: Hambini Village’s journey to transformation
item.description: [PNG Buzz] Thanks to community unity, international support by the Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations (FAO) and visionary leadership, 9,000+ people of Hambini Village in East Sepik Province not only took a path to improve their economic prospects using cocoa but also paved the way for a br

item.title: Australia and PNG partner to enhance digital forensic training for police
item.description: [PNG Facts] A joint effort between Australia and Papua New Guinea has delivered new forensic tools and specialist training to strengthen police investigative capacity in digital crime detection. The Royal Papua New Guinea Constabulary, with support from the Australian Federal Police Pacific, has completed a fiv

item.title: Vipers hand Dabaris first defeat to keep unbeaten streak alive
item.description: [Post Courier] The Moni Plus NCDC Port Moresby Vipers have extended their winning streak to three matches, handing the Gas Resource Central Dabaris their first loss of the season with a commanding 24–10 victory during the Digicel ExxonMobil Cup’s "Men in League" round. In a high-stakes clash between two previously

item.title: Tapini LLG president assures people of safety after murder suspects arrested
item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] BY JOE GURINA LOCAL level government president for Tapini Buni Murua has assured locals that their safety is guaranteed after capturing suspects of the Lilo killing. The suspects who were on the run for over a month after brutal killing of a police reservist from the Tapini police command in Central

item.title: Dean Young appointed interim Head Coach of Dragons
item.description: [The PNG Sun] The St George Illawarra Dragons have confirmed the appointment of Dean Young as interim head coach for the remainder of the season. Young takes over at a challenging time for the club as they look to improve their performances and regain consistency in the competition. Related news: https://www.drag

item.title: La Nina bai kam - gavman mas redi
item.description: [Wantok Niuspepa] National Weather Service (NWS) bilong PNG i tok 'La Nina' bai kam klostu nau tasol i no gat wanpela toksave i kam long gavman yet long wanem samting ol bai mekim o sapos ol i redi long wanem kain ol hevi dispela taim nogut bai nap long bringim.

item.title: PNGFA’s weak response to log export transparency call
item.description: [Act Now PNG] PNGFA has responded weakly to ACT NOW's call for transparency on log export data

item.description: [Bougainville News] Opening Statement by ABG President Hon. Ishmael Toroama, MHR at the Joint Consultation Moderator Meeting Sir Jerry Mateparae, Distinguished Moderator, the Honourable James Marape, Prime Minister of Papua New Guinea, Foreign Missions, distinguished dignitaries, ladies and gentlemen. item.title: ‘The rollout will continue’: Vodafone PNG’s CEO on the telco’s ongoing expansion
item.description: [Business Advantage PNG] Vodafone PNG has built 800 towers since becoming Papua New Guinea’s third telco in 2022. Its Regional CEO Pradeep Lal speaks with Business Advantage PNG about the progress being made in its infrastructure buildout, the impact digitalisation is having in PNG, and plans to enter the mobile money marke

item.title: Lorenzo Moli’s Path to Global Affairs
item.description: [Education News PNG] From the village of Nanuku on Malo Island, Vanuatu, to the global arena of diplomacy and international affairs, Lorenzo Moli’s academic and professional journey is The post Lorenzo Moli’s Path to Global Affairs first appeared on Education News PNG .

item.title: Australia secures fertiliser deal with Indonesia
item.description: [Inside PNG] Australian farmers will receive an additional 250,000 tonnes of fertiliser from Indonesia, as the conflict in the Middle East continues to squeeze supplies. The post Australia secures fertiliser deal with Indonesia first appeared on Inside PNG . The post Australia secures fertiliser deal with Indone

item.title: PNG PM Oversees Defence as Recruitment Investigation Proceeds
item.description: [PNG Facts] Papua New Guinea Prime Minister James Marape has assumed direct authority over the Defence portfolio as scrutiny intensifies over recruitment practices within the military. He announced that Dr Billy Joseph will not be involved in Defence matters for now, as the government awaits the outcome of an o

item.title: Minister Kaeok arrives in Wapenamanda to participate in gun surrender program
item.description: [Post Courier] Transport Minister and Wapenamanda Open MP, Miki Kaeok, arrived in his district today to participate and officially witness the gun surrendering program of two warring factions in his district to be staged tomorrow. The bloody tribal conflict between the Saawalap faction and the Palinau Alliance fac

item.title: PNGDF soldiers seize 460 cartons of beer in Enga operation
item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] THE PapuaNew Guinea Defence Force (PNGDF) personnel operating under the command of Enga Provincial Police Commander has conducted an operation at Laiagam resulting in the seizure of 460 cartons of beer on Wednesday. ENGA PPC Chief Superintendent Steven Harris said the PNGDF soldiers initially encoun

item.title: Enga women lead peace march, call to end tribal fighting
item.description: [The PNG Sun] Women in Enga Province have made a strong stand for peace, carrying out a peaceful march on Tuesday from Mambisanda through Wapenamanda Station and into Wabag Town. The march was organized by the PNG Council of Churches Christian Women, who walked together with one clear message: “Lusim Gun, Kisim B

item.title: Call on PNG Forest Authority to publish log export data
item.description: [Act Now PNG] ACT NOW! is calling on the PNG Forest Authority to publish the complete log export data for 2024 and 2025.

item.title: Bougainville News Alerts : Speaker of the House of Representatives Simon Pentanu, has announced the official dates of the 2025 ABG General Election
item.description: [Bougainville News] Speaker of the House of Representatives Simon Pentanu, has announced the official dates of the 2025 ABG General Election Mr Pentanu said that he would be issuing the writs for the 2025 ABG General Election of the Bougainville House of … Continue reading →

item.title: Four things to see and do in Lae, Papua New Guinea’s second city
item.description: [Business Advantage PNG] Lae is the industrial and manufacturing capital of Papua New Guinea and the gateway to the densely populated Highlands region. Nadav Shemer Shlezinger shares a few top places to go for sightseeing in the coastal city. The post Four things to see and do in Lae, Papua New Guinea’s second city appeared

item.title: Defence strategy downplaying larger threat: ex-ADF head
item.description: [Inside PNG] A former defence chief has warned the military's new strategy ignores some of the biggest threats facing the Australian Defence Force, despite a record spend. The post Defence strategy downplaying larger threat: ex-ADF head first appeared on Inside PNG . The post Defence strategy downplaying larger

item.title: PNG Police Seize Over 1,000 Knives in Port Moresby Crackdown
item.description: [PNG Facts] A major police operation across Port Moresby has resulted in the seizure of more than 1,000 knives as authorities intensify efforts to restore order in the country's busiest city. The haul was uncovered during a three-week enforcement campaign led by police under an initiative aimed at clearing ille

item.title: At First Light: Anzac Day Dawn Services across Papua New Guinea
item.description: [Post Courier] As the first light of Anzac Day broke across Papua New Guinea on Saturday, communities gathered in quiet reflection at sites woven into our shared wartime history. Dawn services were held in places of historic military significance across the country from Port Moresby to Wewak, honouring those who h

item.title: Buk bilong Pikinini renews of partnership with PNG Air
item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] By GINNAH MINI BUK bilong Pikinini (BbP) has announced its three-year renewal of partnership with PNG Air at 6-Mile Library Learning Center, Port Moresby. PNGAir had been supporting BbP since 2018 by providing free tickets to BbP officers to travel around the country where the Local Library Centers

item.title: Swift Police response prevents deadly sorcery violence in Enga
item.description: [The PNG Sun] Police in Enga swiftly have responded to sorcery accusation-related violence to prevent further escalation following the death of a young man at Suyan Camp Gate in Porgera. Enga Provincial Police Commander (PPC), Chief Superintendent Steven Harris, said the man died after consuming alcohol continuou

item.title: Call on PNG Forest Authority to Publish Latest Log Export Data
item.description: [Act Now PNG] ACT NOW! is calling on the PNG Forest Authority to publish the complete log export data for 2024 and 2025.

item.title: PNG Formalises Key Defence Pact with Australia
item.description: [PNG Facts] Papua New Guinea has taken a major step in strengthening its defence cooperation with Australia after Foreign Affairs Minister Justin Tkatchenko signed the Instrument of Ratification for the Puk Puk Treaty in Port Moresby. PNG Formalises Key Defence Pact with Australia The agreement, officially rati

item.title: What's in a bilum?: PNG's iconic string bag
item.description: [Act Now PNG] In Papua New Guinea, the bilum is among the most familiar objects of daily life.

item.title: PNG emergency teams survive Bougainville boat disaster, reach Buka safely
item.description: [PNG Facts] A rescue mission in Bougainville turned into a survival struggle after a speedboat capsized near Ausarara Island, leaving emergency responders stranded at sea before they were brought to safety in Buka, Papua New Guinea. The incident unfolded as a team from Bougainville Disaster and Emergency Servic
